name: Sudan
headline: Sudan FLOOR CONFIRMATION at 0.0 / Critical — UN OHCHR primary-source documentation: 880+ civilians killed by drones in first 4 months of 2026 (UN Human Rights Office). May 12 drone strikes hit Kornoi (North Darfur) market, civilian vehicles, water well — survival-infrastructure destruction. May 8 strikes killed 26 in South Kordofan and North Kordofan. Drone warfare expanding geographically beyond Kordofan/Darfur to Blue Nile, White Nile, Khartoum. May 4 airport strike disrupted all flights — civilian infrastructure disruption at national scale. UN OHCHR Türk 'high alert' designation issued May (new INT-dimension accountability signal). Banjul Joint Declaration (May 12) condemnation continuing. Floor sustained at 0.0; conduct documentation updated with two new conduct categories (geographic-spread, civilian-infrastructure-targeting-at-national-scale).

AWR.rationale: Floor sustained. State-level harm detection absent across SAF and RSF; international monitoring (UN OHCHR, UNHRC fact-finding mission) producing primary-source documentation despite belligerent obstruction.

EMP.rationale: Floor sustained. No state-level empathic engagement with documented civilian-casualty patterns by either belligerent.

ACT.rationale: Floor sustained. AC1 responsiveness: state response is conflict continuation; AC4 resource mobilization: humanitarian-access obstruction by both parties continues.

EQU.rationale: Floor sustained. EQ4 inclusion: ethnic-cleansing pattern in Darfur (El Geneina, sustained UN expert panel findings); drone warfare geographic expansion (Blue Nile, White Nile, Khartoum) extends harm distribution.

BND.rationale: Floor sustained. B3 scope: Kornoi water well strike is survival-infrastructure destruction (water-source-targeting is a distinct civilian-harm category under IHL); airport strike is national-infrastructure-disruption category.

ACC.rationale: Floor sustained. AB2 correction willingness: no documented internal review by either belligerent; AB4 reparation: zero mechanism. ICC active investigation continues with sealed warrants reportedly issued. Banjul Joint Declaration (AU/ACHPR, May 12) external accountability signal.

SYS.rationale: Floor sustained. S1 causal reasoning: drone warfare expansion is a systems-level capability investment in civilian-strike capacity (UN: 80%+ of deaths attributed to drone strikes, 880+ in 4 months); S2 long-term impact: survival-infrastructure destruction (water wells, airports) compounds humanitarian crisis.

INT.rationale: Floor sustained. I1 consistency under pressure: SAF-RSF civil war producing world's largest displacement crisis (>10M displaced); no integrity-restoration trajectory. UN OHCHR 'high alert' May designation is independent integrity signal documenting trajectory deterioration.

evidence.summary: UN OHCHR press release: Türk issues high alert on widening Sudan conflict amid increased use of drones. 880+ civilians killed by drones January-April 2026.
UN News story: drone warfare expansion and civilian-infrastructure targeting documented.
Euronews May 11: Sudan drone strikes killed at least 880 civilians between January and April 2026, UN says.
ACHPR Banjul Joint Declaration on Sudan (May 12) — African continental accountability signal.

keyEvidence: UN OHCHR May 2026: 880+ civilians killed by drones January-April 2026 (primary-source documentation).
May 12 2026: Kornoi (North Darfur) drone strikes on market, civilian vehicles, water well — survival-infrastructure destruction.
May 8 2026: Drone strikes killed 26 in South Kordofan and North Kordofan.
May 4 2026: Khartoum International airport strike disrupted all flights — civilian infrastructure disruption at national scale.
Geographic spread of drone warfare to Blue Nile, White Nile, Khartoum — conflict no longer regionalized.
UN OHCHR Türk 'high alert' designation (May 2026) — new international integrity signal.
ACHPR Banjul Joint Declaration (May 12) — regional accountability signal.
rationale: Floor sustained. Two new conduct categories added since May 13 cycle: (1) geographic-spread of drone warfare beyond Kordofan/Darfur to Blue Nile, White Nile, Khartoum — conflict de-regionalization; (2) survival-infrastructure targeting at national scale (Kornoi water well + Khartoum airport). UN OHCHR 880+ civilian-deaths-by-drone designation is the densest primary-source single-window casualty documentation in the floor-cluster countries record for 2026. Floor-exit criteria not in trajectory.
watches: Banjul Joint Declaration follow-on enforcement steps
carryForward: UN HRC fact-finding mission drone-warfare documentation (May 13 cycle)
Banjul Joint Declaration (May 12) — carry-over from May 13
RSF violations 'particularly widespread and systematic' (UN, carry-over)
newConductCategoryNote: Two new conduct sub-categories documented this cycle: (1) survival-infrastructure-targeting-at-national-scale (water wells + airport in single 10-day window); (2) drone-warfare-geographic-de-regionalization (conflict spread to Blue Nile, White Nile, Khartoum). The water-well-targeting category is methodologically significant — it isolates a specific IHL-protected target class for documentation in the floor-cluster.

math_check.validator_check: Floor maintained. May 14 conduct documentation does not affect composite arithmetic.

disclaimer: This assessment is based on publicly available information and does not constitute a formal Compassion Benchmark Certified Assessment.
